prompts.title: Experimental Design with Power Analysis
prompts.prompt: Act as a senior research scientist at CSIR-NCL Pune with 12 years of designing biochemical assays. I will paste my hypothesis. Design the experiment covering: (1) variables (independent / dependent / control), (2) sample size with power analysis (G*Power calc for effect size 0.5, alpha 0.05, power 0.8), (3) replicates (technical + biological), (4) controls (positive, negative, vehicle), (5) randomization scheme, (6) blinding protocol, (7) statistical test plan, (8) failure / abort criteria. Constraints: align with NIH guidelines; cite ARRIVE 2.0 if animal model; CDSCO / IAEC if applicable; reagent traceability; document in lab notebook + ELN.

prompts.title: Standard Operating Procedure for Assay
prompts.prompt: Act as a quality manager at NABL-accredited research lab. Write a complete SOP for ELISA assay (96-well plate, sandwich format) for measuring inflammatory cytokine. Cover: (1) purpose + scope, (2) materials with vendor + part numbers + storage, (3) reagent prep + safety, (4) step-by-step procedure (with timing + rest tolerance), (5) quality control (positive + negative + standard curve), (6) acceptance criteria (CV<15%, R²>0.98), (7) data recording in LIMS, (8) troubleshooting (5 common issues), (9) version + sign-off. Constraints: align with NABL ISO/IEC 17025 + ICMR-NIN; SOP version-controlled; bilingual where possible (lab Hindi/English); audit-trail-ready.

prompts.title: Results Interpretation for Western Blot
prompts.prompt: Act as a senior cell biologist with 14 years of Western blot interpretation. I will paste an image description + densitometry numbers. Interpret: (1) protein expression changes vs control (% increase / decrease, statistical significance), (2) loading control normalisation, (3) molecular weight verification, (4) artefact check (smearing, ladder, non-specific bands), (5) reproducibility across n=3 biological replicates, (6) caveats / limitations, (7) implications for hypothesis, (8) suggested follow-up experiments. Constraints: cite seminal references; respect figure-integrity standards (no over-adjustment); raw image + metadata archived; align with Cell Press / Nature image policy.

prompts.title: Lab Report Structuring (IMRaD)
prompts.prompt: Act as a research writer at TIFR Mumbai. Structure my lab report on a synthesis project. I will paste raw observations. Build a full IMRaD report: (1) title (specific, not generic), (2) abstract (250 words), (3) introduction (background + gap + objective), (4) materials & methods (reproducible), (5) results (data + figures + tables, statistical analysis), (6) discussion (interpret + compare + limitations), (7) conclusion (1 paragraph), (8) acknowledgements + contributions + COI, (9) references. Constraints: align with thesis report norms; figures + tables numbered; cite at least 25 papers; raw data archived on Mendeley / Zenodo with DOI; CRediT taxonomy for contributions.

prompts.title: Safety Assessment for Chemical Synthesis
prompts.prompt: Act as a chemical-safety officer (CISH-trained) with 10 years at a synthesis lab. Write a safety assessment for a new synthetic procedure I'm planning (involves NaH, DMF reflux, and a CO2 release step). Cover: (1) MSDS for each chemical, (2) hazards (corrosivity, reactivity, exotherm risk), (3) PPE required, (4) engineering controls (fume hood, blast shield, secondary containment), (5) emergency response (spill, fire, exposure), (6) waste-disposal protocol, (7) JSA / HIRA matrix, (8) sign-off + buddy system, (9) training certification of operator. Constraints: align with Indian PESO + Factories Act (Cl. 41); WHO GHS labelling; respect IISc / CSIR best practices; document for IAEC equivalent for chemicals.

prompts.title: Lab Notebook Discipline Coach
prompts.prompt: Act as a research integrity officer who has reviewed 200+ lab notebooks during PhD evaluations. Coach me on lab-notebook discipline. Cover: (1) format (date + experiment ID + objective + procedure + observation + conclusion), (2) ELN tools recommended (Benchling, LabArchives, OneNote — pros/cons), (3) what to include (raw data, mistakes, pivots, hand-drawn diagrams), (4) what NOT to do (correction fluid, page tearing, retroactive entries), (5) cross-referencing (sample IDs, instrument logs, inventory), (6) supervisor sign-off cadence, (7) data-integrity assurance (FAIR principles), (8) backup + version control, (9) IP-protection logic (timestamped entries). Constraints: align with UGC research integrity guidelines + DPDP; auditable for 10 years; institute-property handover at exit.

prompts.title: Microscopy Image Quantification
prompts.prompt: Act as an imaging scientist with 11 years of confocal + super-resolution work. Build a quantification protocol for fluorescence microscopy images. I will paste sample images + research question. Cover: (1) ImageJ / Fiji workflow with macro, (2) thresholding strategy (Otsu / manual / triangle), (3) ROI selection (manual / segmentation), (4) measurement parameters (intensity, area, count, colocalisation Pearson / Manders), (5) batch processing for n=30 images, (6) statistical analysis (paired t-test / mixed model), (7) blinded analysis protocol, (8) figure preparation (look-up tables, scale bars, panel layout). Constraints: align with figure-integrity policies (Nature, Cell); raw .czi / .nd2 files archived; macro version-controlled on GitHub.

prompts.title: Reagent Inventory + Procurement
prompts.prompt: Act as a lab manager at IIT Bombay chemistry department with 9 years. Build a reagent inventory + procurement system. Cover: (1) inventory database schema (chemical name, CAS, brand, lot, expiry, location, hazard class, reorder threshold), (2) barcode + QR labelling, (3) FIFO usage, (4) procurement workflow (indent → quote → PO → receipt), (5) GFR-compliant vendor selection (3 quotes for >₹25K), (6) cold-chain reagents storage, (7) waste log + return-to-vendor for surplus, (8) annual inventory audit. Constraints: align with IIT financial code + GeM portal where applicable; INR with GST + customs duty for imports; Form 49 customs paperwork; UNICOM / SUITE compatibility.

prompts.title: Statistical Test Selection Guide
prompts.prompt: Act as a biostatistician with 12 years at AIIMS / NIMHANS. Help me select the right statistical test for my data. I will paste: study design + data type + sample size + research question. Recommend: (1) primary test (parametric / non-parametric with rationale), (2) assumptions check (normality, homoscedasticity, independence, sphericity), (3) alternative test if assumption fails, (4) effect size measure (d / r / η²), (5) post-hoc / multiple comparison correction, (6) confidence intervals, (7) software (R / SPSS / SAS / GraphPad / JASP) with code snippet, (8) reporting format (APA 7 / CONSORT / STROBE). Constraints: align with field reporting standards; flag statistical fallacies (HARKing, p-hacking); pre-register on OSF where possible.

prompts.title: Cell Culture Contamination Troubleshoot
prompts.prompt: Act as a senior cell-biology scientist. My HeLa cell culture shows opacity + pH shift after 36 hrs. Diagnose: (1) likely cause (bacterial vs fungal vs yeast vs mycoplasma), (2) immediate quarantine actions, (3) test protocols (Gram stain, mycoplasma PCR, fungal culture), (4) media + antibiotic check (penicillin-streptomycin, plasmocin), (5) source-of-contamination audit (incubator, hood, water bath, BSC, operators, reagents, frozen stocks), (6) decontamination protocol for shared equipment, (7) downstream impact on ongoing experiments, (8) prevention plan (mycoplasma testing every 30 days, single-use pipettes for shared cells). Constraints: align with ATCC / NCCS Pune protocols; document chain-of-custody for cell line; respect biosafety BSL-2.

prompts.title: Animal Ethics Application (CPCSEA)
prompts.prompt: Act as a senior animal-ethics committee member. Draft a CPCSEA / IAEC ethics application for a study using 24 Wistar rats to test a novel anti-diabetic compound. Cover: (1) scientific justification + literature backing, (2) species choice + sample size (3R principles — replace, reduce, refine), (3) protocol details (dose, route, duration, endpoints), (4) anaesthesia + analgesia, (5) humane endpoints, (6) personnel + training, (7) housing + enrichment, (8) waste + carcass disposal, (9) data + tissue archiving, (10) PI + co-investigator declarations. Constraints: align with CPCSEA Form B + ARRIVE 2.0; minimum-pain principle; ethics committee meeting timeline (45 days from submission); approval mandatory before procurement.

prompts.title: Conference Poster Layout Brief
prompts.prompt: Act as a senior researcher who has won best-poster awards at IISc / IIT conferences. Build a poster brief for my conference submission. Cover: (1) layout (left-to-right flow, 4-5 columns), (2) section sizes (intro 15%, method 20%, results 40%, discussion + conclusion 25%), (3) typography (heading 60pt, body 28pt, smallest 24pt), (4) colour palette (3 max, accessible), (5) figure / image priority (one hero finding visible from 6 feet), (6) self-explanatory headings, (7) QR code linking to paper / data, (8) take-home message in 1 sentence (visible from 10 feet), (9) printing logistics (Bengaluru / Hyderabad / Mumbai vendor with INR rates). Constraints: A0 size standard; printed locally to save courier; aim 30-second-readable; align with conference template if specified.

prompts.title: GitHub Repository for Research Code
prompts.prompt: Act as a research software engineer with 7 years of best-practice repos for academic code. Build the structure for my GitHub repo accompanying a research paper. Cover: (1) directory layout (src/, data/, notebooks/, results/, paper/), (2) README (project, install, usage, data, results, citation, license), (3) requirements.txt / environment.yml, (4) Makefile or DVC pipeline, (5) tests + CI (GitHub Actions), (6) data versioning (Git LFS / DVC / Zenodo with DOI), (7) reproducibility (Docker / Singularity), (8) license (MIT / Apache 2 / GPL with rationale), (9) DOI minting via Zenodo, (10) CITATION.cff. Constraints: align with FAIR principles + UGC open-science; ensure paper-result reproducibility from clean clone; raw data archived externally if >2GB.

prompts.title: Reagent Stability + Shelf-Life Study
prompts.prompt: Act as a formulation scientist at Biocon with 9 years. Design a 12-month shelf-life study for a novel buffer reagent. Cover: (1) study design (n=60 vials, 3 storage conditions: 4°C, -20°C, 25°C), (2) sampling timepoints (0, 1, 3, 6, 9, 12 months), (3) analytical parameters (pH, conductivity, microbial, potency, appearance), (4) acceptance criteria, (5) statistical model (Arrhenius + linear regression for shelf-life prediction), (6) ICH Q1A(R2) alignment, (7) test methods + their validation, (8) stability indicating analytical method, (9) reporting + label claim. Constraints: align with CDSCO + ICH for biopharma; raw data archived 5 years; OOS investigation protocol; vendor-validated reference standards.

prompts.title: Conference Talk (15 Min) Slide Plan
prompts.prompt: Act as a senior scientist who has given 30+ conference talks. Build a 15-min talk slide plan for an Indian National Science Congress / DAE-BRNS symposium presentation. Cover: (1) slide 1 (title + funding), (2) slide 2 (acknowledgements), (3) slide 3 (motivation in 1 minute), (4) slides 4-5 (background + gap), (5) slides 6-9 (methodology), (6) slides 10-13 (key results — one finding per slide), (7) slide 14 (limitations + future work), (8) slide 15 (conclusion + thank-you). For each: 1-line message + visual hierarchy + simple animation cue. Constraints: 1 minute per slide rule; readable at last row; cite figure sources; allow 3-4 minutes for audience Q&A.
